assumptions: On every change in the input signal (IN) or positive edge of CLK or RST, if RST is active, the output signal MATCH is set to 0.

If the sequence of inputs IN is 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, the MATCH signal is 1 at the same time as the last occurrence of IN=1. If the sequence of inputs IN is 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, the MATCH signal becomes 1 at the fifth and ninth of IN; otherwise, it is set to 0.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.

checks.description: After reset release, observable outputs should reach a stable externally visible state before functional checking resumes.
notes: Reset functional oracle does not invent specific reset values.
checks.pass_condition: Observable outputs settle to a stable, externally usable state without relying on undocumented exact-cycle convergence.
checks.trigger_condition: When reset is released after a valid assertion interval.

checks.description: Observe externally visible state progress after a legal operation.
notes: Sequential functional oracle remains event-based unless a future contract explicitly provides fixed latency.
checks.pass_condition: At least one observable output or progress indicator eventually updates in a manner consistent with state progress; no exact-cycle update is required.
checks.trigger_condition: When the linked plan applies one or more legal sequential operations.

checks.description: Observe that MATCH transitions to 1 exactly on the clock cycle where the fifth bit of the pattern 10011 is presented, and returns to 0 otherwise.
checks.pass_condition: After reset release, when the input stream contains the subsequence 1,0,0,1,1, the MATCH output becomes 1 on the same cycle as the final '1' and stays 0 for all other cycles.
checks.trigger_condition: When a five‑bit sliding window of IN matches 10011.

checks.description: Do not infer business-level completion or acceptance solely from reset activity.
notes: Clock/reset remain anchors and are not emitted as business outputs.
checks.pass_condition: No safety property is violated by treating reset as control-only; later functional claims require separate post-reset evidence.
checks.trigger_condition: While reset is asserted or being released.

checks.description: Guardrail: avoid fixed-cycle expectations unless the contract later proves them.
notes: Default property oracle protects against over-specific later render behavior.
checks.pass_condition: The oracle only requires conservative, externally visible progress or stability and forbids hidden exact-cycle assumptions.
checks.trigger_condition: Whenever the linked case evaluates externally visible progress.

checks.description: Guardrail: avoid fixed-cycle expectations unless the contract later proves them.
notes: Default property oracle protects against over-specific later render behavior.
checks.pass_condition: The oracle only requires conservative, externally visible progress or stability and forbids hidden exact-cycle assumptions.
checks.trigger_condition: Whenever the linked case evaluates externally visible progress.

checks.description: MATCH must be asserted only when the input pattern 1-0-0-1-1 completes, and deasserted otherwise.
checks.pass_condition: For every clock cycle, MATCH == 1 iff the last five sampled IN bits equal 1,0,0,1,1; otherwise MATCH == 0. No assumption of additional latency beyond the same cycle as the final input bit.
checks.trigger_condition: When a five‑bit window of IN ending on the current cycle matches 10011.

checks.description: Guardrail: repeated operations must not collapse into a single ambiguous completion claim.
notes: Stability-style property used instead of throughput-specific claims.
checks.pass_condition: Observed progress remains order-aware and non-contradictory even when exact queueing semantics are unresolved.
checks.trigger_condition: When the linked case applies repeated or back-to-back operations.

checks.description: Guardrail: illegal or constrained inputs do not require normal completion or success signaling.
notes: Negative behavior is guarded by explicit contract constraints only.
checks.pass_condition: The oracle only requires safe non-violation behavior and does not invent undocumented error responses.
checks.trigger_condition: When the linked plan probes documented illegal or constrained inputs.

checks.description: Guardrail: avoid fixed-cycle expectations unless the contract later proves them.
notes: Default property oracle protects against over-specific later render behavior.
checks.pass_condition: The oracle only requires conservative, externally visible progress or stability and forbids hidden exact-cycle assumptions.
checks.trigger_condition: Whenever the linked case evaluates externally visible progress.

assumptions: Handshake groups are treated as heuristic anchors, not fully proven protocol specifications.

checks.description: Treat reset assertion/release as a control event and require reset-safe interface behavior.
notes: Reset is used as an anchor only; it is not treated as a business output.
checks.pass_condition: No acceptance, completion, or externally visible functional progress is claimed solely during reset; post-release observation waits for stable, externally visible behavior.
checks.trigger_condition: When reset 'RST' is asserted and later released according to the inferred polarity.

notes: Reset oracle focuses on control-safe behavior instead of value-specific output claims.
